req.title: Hr-Admin views timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-020 requires an hr-admin to view a timesheet via UI with mandatory field validation and audit record creation. Test coverage spans: (1) EP positive path – valid hr-admin views a timesheet and audit record is written; (2) NEG – unauthenticated user receives 401; (3) NEG/DT – authenticated user with wrong role (employee) receives 403; (4) BVA – timesheet_id at minimum boundary value (1) is accepted; (5) NEG – missing mandatory field returns 400. This covers all stated error pathways and the success path, satisfying the minimum positive/negative/boundary requirement.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_timesheet_success
preconditions: A valid timesheet with known ID exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /timesheets and submit view form with valid timesheet_id
Capture HTTP response and rendered page content
Query audit log for the triggered event
expected: Response body contains entity id matching requested timesheet
Timesheet details are rendered on screen
Audit record for view action is written
trace: R-WEB-020: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_views_timesheet_success(hr_admin_session, existing_timesheet_id, audit_log_reader):
    page = hr_admin_session
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    <helper_fn: fill_timesheet_view_form>(page, timesheet_id=existing_timesheet_id)
    response = <helper_fn: submit_and_capture_response>(page)
    assert response.status in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(existing_timesheet_id)
    assert <helper_fn: page_shows_timesheet_details>(page, existing_timesheet_id)
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_entry(action="view_timesheet", entity_id=existing_timesheet_id)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet
preconditions: No active session / user is unauthenticated
actions: Ensure no authentication cookies or tokens are present via <fixture: anonymous_session>
Attempt GET /timesheets with a valid timesheet_id query param
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet(anonymous_session, existing_timesheet_id, audit_log_reader):
    page = anonymous_session
    response = <helper_fn: raw_get>(page, f"/timesheets?id={existing_timesheet_id}")
    assert response.status == 401
    body = response.json()
    assert "unauthenticated" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "unauthorized" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_entry(action="view_timesheet", entity_id=existing_timesheet_id)
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_timesheet
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not hr-admin)
A valid timesheet with known ID exists
actions: Navigate to /timesheets and attempt to view timesheet with valid timesheet_id
Capture HTTP response status and body
tests.executable:
def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_timesheet(employee_session, existing_timesheet_id, audit_log_reader):
    page = employee_session
    response = <helper_fn: raw_get>(page, f"/timesheets?id={existing_timesheet_id}")
    assert response.status == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "forbidden" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "permission" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_entry(action="view_timesheet", entity_id=existing_timesheet_id)
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written when role is forbidden"

tests.name: test_view_timesheet_with_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: A timesheet with ID equal to the minimum valid value (1) exists
actions: Navigate to /timesheets and submit view form with timesheet_id=1 (minimum boundary)
Capture HTTP response and page content
Query audit log for the event
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
Audit record is written for entity id 1
trace: R-WEB-020: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
MINIMUM_TIMESHEET_ID = 1

def test_view_timesheet_with_minimum_boundary_id(hr_admin_session, audit_log_reader):
    page = hr_admin_session
    <helper_fn: ensure_timesheet_exists>(timesheet_id=MINIMUM_TIMESHEET_ID)
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    <helper_fn: fill_timesheet_view_form>(page, timesheet_id=MINIMUM_TIMESHEET_ID)
    response = <helper_fn: submit_and_capture_response>(page)
    assert response.status in (200, 201)
    body = response.json()
    assert int(body["id"]) == MINIMUM_TIMESHEET_ID
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_entry(action="view_timesheet", entity_id=MINIMUM_TIMESHEET_ID)
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written for boundary ID=1"

tests.name: test_view_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Submit view form with timesheet_id field left blank (mandatory field omitted)
Capture HTTP response status and validation messages
expected: Response or page contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
trace: R-WEB-020: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_view_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(hr_admin_session, audit_log_reader):
    page = hr_admin_session
    page.goto("/timesheets")
    <helper_fn: fill_timesheet_view_form>(page, timesheet_id="")
    response = <helper_fn: submit_and_capture_response>(page)
    assert response.status == 400
    body = response.json()
    errors = body.get("errors", body.get("error", ""))
    assert errors, "Validation error details must be returned for missing mandatory field"
    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_entry(action="view_timesheet")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written when validation fails"
